Package org.apache.juneau.http.response

Class BasicHttpException

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Throwable
java.lang.Exception
java.lang.RuntimeException
org.apache.juneau.http.response.BasicHttpException
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, HttpResponseMessage
Direct Known Subclasses:
BadGateway, BadRequest, Conflict, ExpectationFailed, FailedDependency, Forbidden, GatewayTimeout, Gone, HttpVersionNotSupported, InsufficientStorage, InternalServerError, LengthRequired, Locked, LoopDetected, MethodNotAllowed, MisdirectedRequest, NetworkAuthenticationRequired, NotAcceptable, NotExtended, NotFound, NotImplemented, PayloadTooLarge, PreconditionFailed, PreconditionRequired, RangeNotSatisfiable, RequestHeaderFieldsTooLarge, ServiceUnavailable, TooManyRequests, Unauthorized, UnavailableForLegalReasons, UnprocessableEntity, UnsupportedMediaType, UpgradeRequired, UriTooLong, VariantAlsoNegotiates
public class BasicHttpException extends RuntimeException implements HttpResponseMessage
Base class for HTTP error response exceptions (4xx and 5xx) in the org.apache.juneau.http stack.

Extends RuntimeException while also implementing HttpResponseMessage, so that HTTP error responses can be both thrown as exceptions and used as response objects. Provides classic-style fluent setters that mutate state and return this, plus an setUnmodifiable() latch that locks the instance against further changes.

Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

See Also:
  • juneau-ng REST client
Since:
9.2.1
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Details

    • BasicHttpException

      public BasicHttpException(int statusCode, String reasonPhrase)
      Constructor with no message body.
      Parameters:
      statusCode - The HTTP status code.
      reasonPhrase - The reason phrase (e.g. "Internal Server Error"). May be null.

    • BasicHttpException

      public BasicHttpException(int statusCode, String reasonPhrase, Throwable cause)
      Constructor with a status code and a cause.
      Parameters:
      statusCode - The HTTP status code.
      reasonPhrase - The reason phrase. May be null.
      cause - The cause. May be null.

    • BasicHttpException

      public BasicHttpException(int statusCode, String reasonPhrase, String msg, Object... args)
      Constructor with a MessageFormat- or String.format-style message body.
      Parameters:
      statusCode - The HTTP status code.
      reasonPhrase - The reason phrase. May be null.
      msg - The exception message and response body. May be null. Treated as a format pattern when args is non-empty. Supports both MessageFormat ({0}) and String.format (%s) placeholders.
      args - Optional message arguments.

    • BasicHttpException

      public BasicHttpException(int statusCode, String reasonPhrase, Throwable cause, String msg, Object... args)
      Constructor with a cause and a MessageFormat- or String.format-style message body.
      Parameters:
      statusCode - The HTTP status code.
      reasonPhrase - The reason phrase. May be null.
      cause - The cause. May be null.
      msg - The exception message. May be null. Treated as a format pattern when args is non-empty. Supports both MessageFormat ({0}) and String.format (%s) placeholders.
      args - Optional message arguments.

    • BasicHttpException

      protected BasicHttpException(BasicHttpException copyFrom)
      Copy constructor.
      Parameters:
      copyFrom - The instance to copy. Must not be null.

  • Method Details

    • getStatusLine

      public HttpStatusLine getStatusLine()
      Description copied from interface: HttpResponseMessage
      Returns the status line of this response.
      Specified by:
      getStatusLine in interface HttpResponseMessage
      Returns:
      The status line. Never null.

    • getHeaders

      public List<HttpHeader> getHeaders()
      Description copied from interface: HttpResponseMessage
      Returns all response headers, in the order they were received.
      Specified by:
      getHeaders in interface HttpResponseMessage
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable list of response headers. Never null.

      Returns the response body, or null if the response has no body.

      getBody in interface HttpResponseMessage
      Returns:
      The response body, possibly null.

      public BasicHttpException setStatusLine(HttpStatusLine value)
      Sets the status line.
      Parameters:
      value - New status line. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • setStatusCode

      public BasicHttpException setStatusCode(int value)
      Sets the status code, preserving the existing protocol version and reason phrase.
      Parameters:
      value - New status code.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • setReasonPhrase

      public BasicHttpException setReasonPhrase(String value)
      Sets the reason phrase, preserving the existing protocol version and status code.
      Parameters:
      value - New reason phrase. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • setProtocolVersion

      public BasicHttpException setProtocolVersion(HttpProtocolVersion value)
      Sets the protocol version, preserving the existing status code and reason phrase.
      Parameters:
      value - New protocol version. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • setHeaders

      public BasicHttpException setHeaders(List<HttpHeader> values)
      Replaces all headers with the given list.
      Parameters:
      values - New headers. null is treated as an empty list.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • setHeaders

      public BasicHttpException setHeaders(HttpHeader... values)
      Replaces all headers with the given array.
      Parameters:
      values - New headers. null is treated as an empty array.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • setHeader

      public BasicHttpException setHeader(HttpHeader value)
      Sets a single header, replacing any existing header with the same name.
      Parameters:
      value - The header to set. null is ignored.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • setHeader

      public BasicHttpException setHeader(String name, String value)
      Sets a single header, replacing any existing header with the same name.
      Parameters:
      name - Header name. Must not be null.
      value - Header value. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • addHeader

      public BasicHttpException addHeader(HttpHeader value)
      Appends a header to the end of the header list.
      Parameters:
      value - The header to add. null is ignored.
      Returns:
      This object.

      public BasicHttpException addHeader(String name, String value)
      Appends a header to the end of the header list.
      Parameters:
      name - Header name.
      value - Header value.
      Returns:
      This object.

      Returns:
      This object.

      value - Body text. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

      public BasicHttpException setContent(HttpBody value)

      value - New body. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • setUnmodifiable

      public BasicHttpException setUnmodifiable()
      Latches this instance so that subsequent calls to mutating setters throw IllegalStateException.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • isUnmodifiable

      public boolean isUnmodifiable()
      Returns whether this instance is locked against modification.
      Returns:
      true if setUnmodifiable() has been called.

    • assertModifiable

      protected final void assertModifiable()
      Throws an IllegalStateException if this instance has been marked unmodifiable.

    • getRootCause

      public Throwable getRootCause()
      Returns the root cause of this exception.

      The root cause is the first exception in the init-cause parent chain that's not one of the following:

      • BasicHttpException
      • InvocationTargetException
      Returns:
      The root cause of this exception, or null if no root cause was found.

    • getFullStackMessage

      public String getFullStackMessage(boolean scrubForXssVulnerabilities)
      Returns all error messages from all errors in this exception's cause chain joined into a single string.
      Parameters:
      scrubForXssVulnerabilities - If true, replaces <, >, and & characters with spaces.
      Returns:
      A multi-line string of messages. Never null.

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Throwable
